{
  "batch": "Package Organization",
  "batch_index": 2,
  "assessments": {
    "package_organization": 74.0
  },
  "dimension_notes": {
    "package_organization": {
      "evidence": [
        "holistic_context.structure shows very large generated directories: goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/ has 49 files and goseg/web/_app/immutable/entry/ has 31 files, both zoned generated.",
        "Assigned generated files are hash-addressed and ownership-obscuring (for example chunks/0.f72efb1a.js, 1.0d44cf41.js, 10.35bd59a0.js), while functional source for behavior exists elsewhere (ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/*.js), splitting runtime ownership across build outputs and source trees.",
        "ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/ is a flat 17-file directory (15 production + 2 tests) containing heterogeneous domains (auth, setup, urbit, network, support, system) plus shared infrastructure (context.js).",
        "context.test.js is colocated with all command implementations in the same high-count folder, increasing mixed-concern navigation inside an already overloaded directory."
      ],
      "impact_scope": "subsystem",
      "fix_scope": "architectural_change",
      "confidence": "high"
    }
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "dimension": "package_organization",
      "identifier": "generated_bundle_ownership_blur",
      "summary": "Committed hashed bundle files create a parallel package tree that obscures ownership boundaries.",
      "related_files": [
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/0.f72efb1a.js",
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/1.0d44cf41.js",
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/10.35bd59a0.js",
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/entry/(home)-layout.svelte.443ba7fd.js",
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/entry/(home)-page.svelte.3cdaa764.js",
        "goseg/web/_app/immutable/entry/_layout.js.984db11e.js"
      ],
      "evidence": [
        "Assigned chunk files are hash-named wrappers/minified modules with no domain semantics, so directory navigation does not reflect feature ownership.",
        "Entry files import hashed chunk modules (for example (home)-page imports multiple ../chunks/*.js artifacts), reinforcing dependency mapping at artifact level instead of source-level package boundaries.",
        "structure profile reports 80 generated files overall, with the two immutable directories dominating file count, indicating package surface is weighted toward build artifacts."
      ],
      "suggestion": "Staged reorg plan: (1) create explicit artifact boundary folders such as ui/build/client/ and goseg/web/static/client/ (or a single canonical artifact root), (2) move immutable output generation target to that boundary and stop treating hashed artifacts as source-owned package content, (3) keep feature ownership in source folders only (ui/src/routes, ui/src/lib/*) and retain only manifest + deployment-required outputs in goseg, (4) update import/build wiring via bundler output config and backend static-path config, then run `npm --prefix ui run build`, `rg \"_app/immutable\" -n`, and project test/build commands to validate no runtime path regressions.",
      "confidence": "high",
      "impact_scope": "codebase",
      "fix_scope": "architectural_change",
      "root_cause_cluster": "artifact_and_source_boundary_misalignment"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "package_organization",
      "identifier": "flat_runtime_commands_overload",
      "summary": "Runtime commands are kept in an overloaded flat directory mixing multiple domains and support layers.",
      "related_files": [
        "ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/auth-commands.js",
        "ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/context.js",
        "ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/context.test.js"
      ],
      "evidence": [
        "commands directory profile has 17 files (>10 threshold) with mixed concerns and layers (domain command modules, shared command context, and tests).",
        "auth-commands.js is domain behavior but depends on generic transport helpers from context.js, yet both live at same package depth without sub-boundaries.",
        "context.test.js sits in the same flat directory as production command modules, increasing folder entropy during feature/domain navigation."
      ],
      "suggestion": "Staged reorg plan: (1) introduce subfolders `ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/core/` (context + shared senders), `ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/domains/` (auth/system/setup/urbit/etc), and `ui/src/lib/runtime/commands/__tests__/` or mirrored test folders, (2) move core first (`context.js`, `context.test.js`) and add temporary barrel exports, (3) move domain modules in small batches and update imports after each batch with `rg \"from './context|from '../commands\" ui/src/lib/runtime -n`, (4) remove temporary barrels once callers are migrated, then validate with `npm --prefix ui test` and `npm --prefix ui run build`.",
      "confidence": "high",
      "impact_scope": "subsystem",
      "fix_scope": "multi_file_refactor",
      "root_cause_cluster": "artifact_and_source_boundary_misalignment"
    }
  ],
  "retrospective": {
    "root_causes": [
      "Build artifacts are allowed to occupy a first-class package surface instead of being isolated behind a deployment artifact boundary.",
      "Runtime command modules grew by accretion in a single folder without a domain/core/testing folder taxonomy."
    ],
    "likely_symptoms": [
      "generated_bundle_ownership_blur",
      "flat_runtime_commands_overload"
    ],
    "possible_false_positives": []
  }
}
